Figure 1: Per-language scaling curves (chrF++, random selection). Top two rows: eng→X; bottom two rows: X→eng.

Table 1: BM25 vs. Rand (chrF++, eng→X, Gemini). Bold: winner. ∆: green = BM25 better, red = Random better.
Table 12: X→eng scaling with random selection (spBLEU). Formatting conventions as described in §C.
Table 7: eng→X results with Llama 3.3 70B (chrF++). Formatting conventions as described in §C.
Figure 2: Bible vs. in-domain examples (chrF++, eng→X, Gemini 2.5 Flash). Bible examples plateau or degrade with more shots, while in-domain examples scale consistently.

Table 13: Effect of example ordering within BM25 and dense retrieval (eng→X, FLORES). Left: spBLEU; right: chrF++. For BM25, “Default” follows the BM25 score ranking; for dense retrieval, “Random” shuffles examples. “Similar/Dissimilar first” places the most/least similar example closest to the query.
Table 11: X→eng scaling with random selection (chrF++). Formatting conventions as described in §C.

Table 3: eng→X results with Gemini 2.5 Flash (chrF++). Formatting conventions as described in §C.
Table 14: Effect of length-based example ordering. Random: no ordering; L2S: Long-to-Short; S2L: Short-to-Long. Pair-L2S/Pair-S2L: ordered by the combined source+target length.
Table 10: eng→X results with Gemma 3 27B (spBLEU). Formatting conventions as described in §C.
